purpose: Evaluate whether conclude turns project evidence into a coherent, bounded scientific story rather than an evidence inventory.

expectedFacts.fact: Within the analysed substantia-nigra astrocyte state axis, QKI and CELF2 were elevated in the protective-like state.
Candidate-guided DTU identified two CELF2 transcript signals and one borderline QKI transcript signal among 27 candidates.
A transcriptome-wide analysis across 12 available donors identified 674 significant transcripts spanning 577 genes.
support: CELF2 ENST00000631460.1 remained significant with FDR=0.012 and log2FC=-3.20

expectedFacts.fact: The DTU foreground was enriched around mitochondrial quality control, autophagy, trafficking, and proteostasis programs.
Event-local reconstruction localized transcript-structure differences for 54 of 55 prioritized genes.
support: 51 loci involved alternative first-exon or transcription-start usage
45 loci involved alternative last-exon or termination usage

expectedFacts.fact: CELF2-compatible evidence was concentrated in a subset of localized mitochondrial-quality-control and autophagy loci.
support: 8 of 54 localized loci reached strict CELF2 motif-compatible support
5 of 16 core genes versus 3 of 38 non-core candidates reached the strict tier

expectedStoryRelationships: The astrocyte state result motivates an RNA-processing question.
The limitations of 3-prime snRNA-seq motivate a move to full-length transcript data.
Candidate DTU motivates the transcriptome-wide test.
The final model integrates these layers without claiming that one factor causes Parkinson's disease.
claimLimits: The evidence does not prove that QKI, CELF2, or a single splicing factor drives Parkinson's disease.
Motif compatibility is not equivalent to direct binding or functional causality.
Three-prime snRNA-seq does not resolve complete transcript isoforms.
Candidate and donor-level findings must retain their stated power and validation limits.
The strongest supported conclusion is an RNA-processing-linked transcript-architecture model with bounded mechanistic interpretation.
requiredManuscriptSignals: A Results sequence that follows the expected scientific relationships
Figure or table anchors for central Results claims
A Discussion that separates supported conclusions from validation needs
Verified external citations for field-context statements

hardFailures.description: Visible manuscript prose concatenates artifact, study, or task descriptions instead of synthesizing a result.
Visible prose contains sentence fragments, duplicated punctuation, field labels, or metadata-shaped text.
A central claim contradicts the expected facts, omits decisive available values, or exceeds a claim limit.
A major Results claim has no usable figure, table, or verifiable quantitative anchor when one exists.
An external citation is fabricated, unresolved, or missing from BibTeX.
The manuscript exposes drafting instructions, audit language, future placeholders, or workflow commentary.
The evaluator passes a draft with any hard failure because expected sections or files exist.
